summary: Typosquat of scikit-learn with silent third-party relay through hardcoded API key
details: Package name `sklern` is a one-character deletion of `sklearn` (PyPI `scikit-learn`, the canonical scikit-learn distribution). The package exposes function names that mimic scikit-learn's surface (`linear_regression`, `logistic_regression`, `decision_tree`, `mlp`, `perceptron`, etc.), but the bodies only `print(...)` example code rather than implementing the advertised ML algorithms — divergent API shape from the package it resembles. The public function `get1(prompt)` in `src/sklern/ai_helper.py` transmits caller-supplied prompt content to `api.groq.com` using a hardcoded API key embedded in the module (`Groq(api_key="gsk_Sj4le4Ibbpe1...")` at ai_helper.py:3, re-exported via `__init__.py`). Any caller of `get1` silently leaks their prompt content to a third-party LLM endpoint under an account they did not configure or consent to. The combination of name-confusion against a top-100 package, mimicked-but-empty API, and silent relay of caller data to a hardcoded third-party destination is an intentional supply-chain attack pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
